allies.rationale_text: Lock-in gives switching-costs a structural language for the way coexistence drag and accumulated commitment make later movement harder.
allies.source_quote: The result is not just delayed efficiency; it is accumulated irreversibility.

allies.rationale_text: Anchoring reinforces switching-costs when early frames or commitments make later alternatives feel harder to evaluate fairly.
allies.source_quote: Anchoring is a cognitive bias where too much focus is placed on a single piece of initial information. This initial fact or number becomes a psychological commitment, making subsequent shifts (the switch) difficult to achieve.

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking fights switching-costs by forcing a rebuild from core facts instead of defending the incumbent frame out of inertia.
antagonists.source_quote: First Principles Thinking ... forces a complete cognitive rebuild (high effort switch) rather than relying on previous models.

antagonists.rationale_text: Intellectual-humility weakens switching-costs by making it easier to challenge familiar assumptions and adopt a different viewpoint when the incumbent no longer fits.
antagonists.source_quote: These models demand that thinkers actively challenge assumptions and acknowledge what they don't know. This directly confronts the comfort and consistency provided by existing mental models.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Switching-costs conflicts with optionality when migration lock-in costs eliminate practical branch choices.
structured_tensions.source_quote: switching-costs vs optionality: switching-costs conflicts with optionality (optionality) when migration lock-in costs eliminate practical branch choices.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Switching-costs conflicts with feedback-loops when delayed switching friction hides deteriorating system fit.
structured_tensions.source_quote: switching-costs vs feedback-loops: switching-costs conflicts with feedback-loops (feedback loops) when delayed switching friction hides deteriorating system fit.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Switching-costs conflicts with confirmation-bias when entrenched commitments cause teams to process information to confirm the existing path rather than fairly evaluate alternatives.
structured_tensions.source_quote: switching-costs vs confirmation-bias: switching-costs conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when entrenched commitments cause teams to process information to confirm the existing path rather than fairly evaluate alternatives.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on irreversibility, commitment-friction, and three explicit current-vocabulary tension blocks.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor transition sprawl beyond the raw ally, antagonist, and structured tension surface.
open_questions: Cognitive-load-theory is a source-real ally too, but the retained set favors the stronger incumbent-friction and anti-entrenchment targets.
